questions.stem: Company X reacquires 1,000 shares of its $10 par common stock for $25 per share using the cost method. What is the journal entry?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Debit Treasury Stock $25,000; Credit Cash $25,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Debit Treasury Stock $10,000; Debit APIC $15,000; Credit Cash $25,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Debit Common Stock $10,000; Debit Retained Earnings $15,000; Credit Cash $25,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Debit Treasury Stock $25,000; Credit Common Stock $10,000; Credit APIC $15,000

questions.explanation: Under the cost method (ASC 505-30-30-5), treasury stock is recorded at the reacquisition cost. The entry is simply: Debit Treasury Stock $25,000, Credit Cash $25,000. The treasury stock is presented as a deduction from total stockholders' equity. The par value method would require allocating to Common Stock and APIC.
questions.explanationJa: 原価法では、自己株式は再取得原価で記録する。仕訳は単純に、借方: 自己株式 $25,000、貸方: 現金 $25,000。自己株式は株主資本合計からの控除項目として表示される。なお、額面法では普通株式とAPIC(追加払込資本)への配分が必要となる。

questions.stem: A company declares a 10% stock dividend when it has 100,000 shares outstanding. The par value is $5, the market price is $20, and retained earnings balance is $500,000. What is the effect on retained earnings?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Decrease of $200,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Decrease of $50,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Decrease of $100,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: No effect

questions.explanation: A small stock dividend (less than 20-25% of outstanding shares) is recorded at fair market value per ASC 505-20-30-3. New shares issued: 100,000 × 10% = 10,000 shares. Retained earnings is reduced by: 10,000 shares × $20 market price = $200,000. Common Stock increases by $50,000 (10,000 × $5 par) and APIC increases by $150,000.
questions.explanationJa: 小規模株式配当(発行済株式の20-25%未満)は公正市場価値で記録する。新規発行株式数: 100,000 × 10% = 10,000株。利益剰余金の減少額: 10,000株 × $20(市場価格) = $200,000。普通株式は$50,000(10,000 × $5額面)増加し、APICは$150,000増加する。

questions.stem: What is the primary accounting difference between a large stock dividend (over 25%) and a stock split?

choices.label: A
choices.text: A large stock dividend reduces retained earnings at par value; a stock split has no journal entry (only a memo entry changing par)

choices.label: B
choices.text: A large stock dividend reduces retained earnings at market value; a stock split reduces at par

choices.label: C
choices.text: Both require the same accounting treatment

choices.label: D
choices.text: A stock split requires shareholder approval; a large stock dividend does not

questions.explanation: Under ASC 505-20-30-6, a large stock dividend (typically over 20-25%) is recorded at par value: Debit Retained Earnings, Credit Common Stock for the par value of new shares. A stock split merely changes the par value per share and increases the number of shares; no journal entry is required (only a memorandum notation).
questions.explanationJa: 大規模株式配当(通常20-25%超)は額面価額で記録する: 借方: 利益剰余金、貸方: 普通株式(新規発行株式の額面価額)。一方、株式分割は1株あたりの額面価額を変更し株式数を増加させるだけであり、仕訳は不要である(備忘記録のみ)。

questions.stem: Under ASC 718, at what date is the fair value of a stock option award to an employee measured?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The vesting date

choices.label: B
choices.text: The grant date

choices.label: C
choices.text: The exercise date

choices.label: D
choices.text: The date the employee starts providing service

questions.explanation: ASC 718-10-30-2 requires that the fair value of equity-classified awards be measured at the grant date. This fair value is then recognized as compensation expense over the requisite service period (usually the vesting period). The fair value is not remeasured after the grant date for equity-classified awards.
questions.explanationJa: 持分分類の報酬の公正価値は付与日(grant date)に測定する。この公正価値を必要勤務期間(通常は権利確定期間)にわたって報酬費用として認識する。持分分類の報酬については、付与日以降に公正価値の再測定は行わない。

questions.stem: When a company appropriates (restricts) retained earnings, what is the effect on total stockholders' equity?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Total equity decreases

choices.label: B
choices.text: Total equity increases

choices.label: C
choices.text: No effect on total equity; it is a reclassification within equity

choices.label: D
choices.text: Total equity decreases by the restricted amount

questions.explanation: An appropriation of retained earnings is merely a reclassification within stockholders' equity—from unappropriated retained earnings to appropriated retained earnings. It does not change total equity or total retained earnings in aggregate. It serves as a disclosure mechanism to communicate that certain earnings are not available for dividends.
questions.explanationJa: 利益剰余金の処分(拘束)は、株主資本内での区分変更に過ぎない。未処分利益剰余金から処分済利益剰余金への振替であり、株主資本合計や利益剰余金の総額には影響しない。一定の利益が配当に利用できないことを伝えるための開示手段である。

questions.stem: Which of the following items is reported in other comprehensive income (OCI) under U.S. GAAP?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Unrealized gains on equity securities with readily determinable fair values

choices.label: B
choices.text: Unrealized gains on available-for-sale debt securities

choices.label: C
choices.text: Gains on sale of equipment

choices.label: D
choices.text: Unrealized gains on trading securities

questions.explanation: The four main components of OCI are: (1) unrealized gains/losses on AFS debt securities, (2) foreign currency translation adjustments, (3) pension/OPEB adjustments (prior service cost, net gain/loss), and (4) effective portion of cash flow hedge gains/losses. Equity securities go through net income (ASC 321), and trading securities are also through net income.
questions.explanationJa: OCIの主な4つの構成要素は: (1) AFS負債証券の未実現損益、(2) 外貨換算調整額、(3) 年金/OPEB調整額(過去勤務費用、純損益)、(4) キャッシュフローヘッジの有効部分の損益。持分証券はASC 321により純利益を通じて処理され、売買目的証券も純利益を通じて処理される。

questions.stem: Company Y has net income of $500,000, 100,000 weighted average common shares outstanding, and 10,000 dilutive stock options with an exercise price of $15 (average market price is $25). What is diluted EPS?
questions.explanation: Treasury stock method: Proceeds = 10,000 × $15 = $150,000. Shares repurchased at market price: $150,000 / $25 = 6,000. Incremental dilutive shares: 10,000 - 6,000 = 4,000. Diluted weighted average shares: 100,000 + 4,000 = 104,000. Diluted EPS = $500,000 / 104,000 = $4.81 (rounded).
questions.explanationJa: 自己株式法(treasury stock method)で計算する。行使収入 = 10,000 × $15 = $150,000。市場価格での買戻し株数: $150,000 / $25 = 6,000株。希薄化増加株式数: 10,000 - 6,000 = 4,000株。希薄化後加重平均株式数: 100,000 + 4,000 = 104,000株。希薄化後EPS = $500,000 / 104,000 = $4.81(四捨五入)。

questions.stem: Under ASC 470-20 (as amended by ASU 2020-06), how should a convertible bond issued at par with no beneficial conversion feature be accounted for?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Separate the debt and equity components and allocate proceeds between them

choices.label: B
choices.text: Record the entire proceeds as a liability (single unit of account)

choices.label: C
choices.text: Record entirely as equity

choices.label: D
choices.text: Record at fair value through profit or loss

questions.explanation: ASU 2020-06 simplified the accounting for convertible instruments by eliminating the beneficial conversion feature and cash conversion models. Under the current guidance, most convertible instruments are accounted for as a single unit of account (entirely as debt) unless they contain features requiring bifurcation under ASC 815 or are issued with substantial premiums.
questions.explanationJa: ASU 2020-06は有利転換特性モデルおよび現金転換モデルを廃止し、転換社債の会計を簡素化した。現行指針では、ほとんどの転換社債は単一の会計単位(全額を負債)として処理する。ただし、ASC 815に基づく区分処理が必要な特性を含む場合や、大幅なプレミアムで発行された場合は例外である。

questions.stem: Cumulative preferred stock dividends in arrears should be:

choices.label: A
choices.text: Recorded as a liability

choices.label: B
choices.text: Disclosed in the notes to the financial statements

choices.label: C
choices.text: Deducted from retained earnings

choices.label: D
choices.text: Added to preferred stock on the balance sheet

questions.explanation: Dividends in arrears on cumulative preferred stock are not a liability until declared by the board of directors. However, they must be disclosed in the notes to the financial statements (ASC 505-10-50-5) because they represent a priority claim on future dividends that affects common stockholders' potential distributions.
questions.explanationJa: 累積的優先株式の未払配当金は、取締役会が宣言するまで負債にはならない。ただし、将来の配当に対する優先権を表し普通株主への配分に影響するため、財務諸表の注記で開示が必要である。

questions.stem: Under ASC 718, how can an entity account for forfeitures of stock-based compensation awards?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Must estimate forfeitures at the grant date

choices.label: B
choices.text: Must account for forfeitures as they occur (actual basis only)

choices.label: C
choices.text: Can elect either to estimate forfeitures or account for them as they occur

choices.label: D
choices.text: Forfeitures are ignored in the compensation calculation

questions.explanation: ASU 2016-09 (codified in ASC 718-10-35-3) provides an accounting policy election: entities can either estimate the number of awards expected to be forfeited (and true-up as needed) or recognize forfeitures when they occur. The election must be applied consistently.
questions.explanationJa: ASU 2016-09(ASC 718-10-35-3に編入)は会計方針の選択肢を提供している。企業は、失効が見込まれる報酬数を見積もる方法(必要に応じて事後調整)、または失効発生時に認識する方法のいずれかを選択できる。この選択は一貫して適用しなければならない。

questions.stem: Alpha Corp. reported net income of $600,000 for Year 1. It has 10,000 shares of 8%, $100 par cumulative preferred stock outstanding. Common shares: 200,000 outstanding on January 1, and 40,000 additional shares issued on April 1. What is basic EPS for Year 1?

Beta Corp. has net income of $500,000 and 200,000 common shares outstanding. It also has convertible preferred stock (10,000 shares, $5 annual dividend per share) that is convertible into 25,000 common shares. What is diluted EPS?
questions.explanation: Basic EPS = ($500,000 - $50,000) / 200,000 = $2.25. For diluted EPS (if-converted method): Add back preferred dividends to numerator ($500,000) and add conversion shares to denominator (200,000 + 25,000 = 225,000). Diluted EPS = $500,000 / 225,000 = $2.22. Since $2.22 < $2.25, the conversion is dilutive and included.
questions.explanationJa: 基本EPS = ($500,000 - $50,000) / 200,000 = $2.25。希薄化後EPS(転換仮定法): 分子に優先配当を戻し$500,000、分母に転換株式を加算し225,000。希薄化後EPS = $500,000 / 225,000 = $2.22。$2.22 < $2.25のため転換は希薄化効果があり、計算に含める。

questions.stem: Gamma Corp. holds 5,000 shares of treasury stock acquired at $30 per share (cost method). It reissues 3,000 of these shares at $38 per share. What is the credit to Additional Paid-in Capital from Treasury Stock transactions?
questions.explanation: Under the cost method, when treasury stock is reissued above cost: Cash received = 3,000 × $38 = $114,000. Treasury Stock credit (at cost) = 3,000 × $30 = $90,000. The excess = $114,000 - $90,000 = $24,000 is credited to APIC - Treasury Stock.
questions.explanationJa: 原価法で自己株式を原価超で再発行する場合: 受取現金 = 3,000 × $38 = $114,000。自己株式の貸方(原価) = 3,000 × $30 = $90,000。超過額 = $114,000 - $90,000 = $24,000は資本剰余金(自己株式取引)に貸方計上する。

questions.stem: On January 1, Year 1, Delta Corp. grants 12,000 stock options (fair value $10 per option at grant date) to employees with a 4-year cliff vesting period. Management estimates a 10% forfeiture rate. What is the stock compensation expense for Year 1?
questions.explanation: Expected options to vest = 12,000 × (1 - 10%) = 10,800. Total compensation cost = 10,800 × $10 = $108,000. Annual expense (straight-line over 4-year vesting period) = $108,000 / 4 = $27,000.

questions.stem: Epsilon Corp. had 100,000 common shares outstanding on January 1. On April 1, it issued 20,000 additional shares for cash. On July 1, it declared a 10% stock dividend. What is the weighted average number of shares for EPS computation?
questions.explanation: Stock dividends are treated as if they occurred at the beginning of the year (retroactively applied). Jan 1 - Mar 31: 100,000 × 1.10 × 3/12 = 27,500. Apr 1 - Jun 30: 120,000 × 1.10 × 3/12 = 33,000. Jul 1 - Dec 31: 132,000 × 6/12 = 66,000. Total weighted average = 27,500 + 33,000 + 66,000 = 126,500.

questions.stem: Zeta Corp. holds 2,000 treasury shares acquired at $40 per share (cost method). APIC from prior treasury stock transactions is $10,000. Zeta reissues 1,000 shares at $35 per share. What amount is debited to APIC - Treasury Stock?
questions.explanation: Cash received = 1,000 × $35 = $35,000. Treasury Stock credit (at cost) = 1,000 × $40 = $40,000. Shortfall = $40,000 - $35,000 = $5,000. Under the cost method, this shortfall is debited first to APIC - Treasury Stock (up to available balance). Since $5,000 ≤ $10,000 available, the full $5,000 is debited to APIC - Treasury Stock.
questions.explanationJa: 受取現金 = 1,000 × $35 = $35,000。自己株式の貸方(原価) = 1,000 × $40 = $40,000。不足額 = $40,000 - $35,000 = $5,000。原価法では、不足額はまず資本剰余金(自己株式取引)から借方計上する(利用可能残高まで)。$5,000 <= 利用可能$10,000のため、全額$5,000が資本剰余金から借方計上される。

questions.stem: Eta Corp. has net income of $300,000 and 150,000 weighted average common shares. It has 20,000 outstanding stock options with an exercise price of $50. The average market price of the stock during the year was $40. What is diluted EPS?
questions.explanation: Under the treasury stock method, options are dilutive only when the exercise price is below the average market price. Here, exercise price ($50) > average market price ($40), so the options are out-of-the-money and anti-dilutive. Anti-dilutive securities are excluded from diluted EPS. Diluted EPS = Basic EPS = $300,000 / 150,000 = $2.00.
questions.explanationJa: 自己株式法では、行使価格が平均市場価格を下回る場合にのみオプションは希薄化効果を持つ。本問では行使価格($50) > 平均市場価格($40)であるため、オプションはアウト・オブ・ザ・マネーで反希薄化的である。反希薄化的証券は希薄化後EPSから除外する。希薄化後EPS = 基本EPS = $300,000 / 150,000 = $2.00。

questions.stem: Theta Corp. has total stockholders' equity of $5,000,000. Preferred stock: 20,000 shares, $100 par, 6% cumulative, callable at $105, with 2 years of dividends in arrears. Common stock: 200,000 shares outstanding. What is the book value per common share?
questions.explanation: Preferred stockholders' claim: Liquidation value = 20,000 × $105 = $2,100,000. Dividends in arrears = 20,000 × $100 × 6% × 2 years = $240,000. Total preferred claim = $2,100,000 + $240,000 = $2,340,000. Equity available to common = $5,000,000 - $2,340,000 = $2,660,000. BV per common share = $2,660,000 / 200,000 = $13.30.

questions.stem: Iota Corp. reported net income of $900,000 for Year 1. It had 300,000 shares outstanding for the entire year. On October 1, Year 1, Iota declared a 3-for-1 stock split. What is basic EPS for Year 1?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 260-10-55-12, stock splits are applied retroactively to all periods presented. The weighted average shares are adjusted as if the split occurred at the beginning of the year. WA shares = 300,000 × 3 = 900,000. Basic EPS = $900,000 / 900,000 = $1.00.
questions.explanationJa: 株式分割はEPS計算上、表示されるすべての期間に遡及適用される。加重平均株式数は分割が期首に発生したかのように調整する。加重平均株式数 = 300,000 × 3 = 900,000。基本EPS = $900,000 / 900,000 = $1.00。

questions.stem: Kappa Corp. has the following data: Beginning retained earnings $800,000; Net income $250,000; Cash dividends declared $60,000; Small stock dividend (at market value) $90,000; Prior period adjustment for error correction (net of tax) reducing beginning balance by $50,000. What is ending retained earnings?
questions.explanation: Prior period adjustment restates beginning balance: $800,000 - $50,000 = $750,000 (restated beginning). Ending RE = Restated beginning + Net income - Cash dividends - Stock dividend = $750,000 + $250,000 - $60,000 - $90,000 = $850,000.

questions.stem: Entity A undergoes a quasi-reorganization to eliminate an accumulated deficit of $2,000,000. Entity A has additional paid-in capital (APIC) of $3,000,000. After the quasi-reorganization, what is the balance of retained earnings and APIC?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Retained earnings: $0; APIC: $1,000,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Retained earnings: $2,000,000; APIC: $1,000,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Retained earnings: $0; APIC: $3,000,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Retained earnings: ($2,000,000); APIC: $3,000,000

questions.explanation: In a quasi-reorganization, the accumulated deficit is eliminated by reducing APIC (or revaluing assets). The deficit of $2,000,000 is charged against APIC, reducing it from $3,000,000 to $1,000,000. Retained earnings is reset to $0. The date of the quasi-reorganization must be disclosed for 10 years.
questions.explanationJa: 準更生では、累積欠損金をAPICの減額（または資産の再評価）により消去する。欠損金$2,000,000をAPICから控除し、$3,000,000から$1,000,000に減少。利益剰余金は$0にリセットする。準更生の日付は10年間開示が必要。

questions.stem: Entity B grants 3,000 stock options with graded vesting: 1,000 vest after Year 1, 1,000 after Year 2, and 1,000 after Year 3. The total fair value is $90,000 ($30 per option). If Entity B uses the graded vesting (accelerated) method, how much compensation expense is recognized in Year 1?
questions.explanation: Under the graded vesting (accelerated) method, each tranche is treated as a separate award. Tranche 1 (1,000 options, 1-year vest): $30,000 × 1/1 = $30,000 in Year 1. Tranche 2 (1,000 options, 2-year vest): $30,000 × 1/2 = $15,000 in Year 1. Tranche 3 (1,000 options, 3-year vest): $30,000 × 1/3 = $10,000 in Year 1. Total Year 1 = $30,000 + $15,000 + $10,000 = $55,000.
questions.explanationJa: 段階的確定（加速法）では、各トランシュを別個のアワードとして扱い、それぞれの確定期間にわたりストレートライン法を適用する。トランシュ1（1,000個、1年確定）：$30,000 × 1/1 = $30,000。トランシュ2（1,000個、2年確定）：$30,000 × 1/2 = $15,000。トランシュ3（1,000個、3年確定）：$30,000 × 1/3 = $10,000。Year 1合計 = $30,000 + $15,000 + $10,000 = $55,000。

questions.stem: Entity C grants 5,000 RSUs to an employee. The RSUs vest after 3 years of service and are settled in shares. The fair value at grant date is $20 per unit. Assuming no forfeitures, what is the annual compensation expense?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 718-10-35-2, RSUs settled in shares are equity-classified awards. Total compensation cost = 5,000 × $20 = $100,000, recognized over the 3-year vesting period. Annual expense = $100,000 / 3 = $33,333. The fair value is measured at the grant date and is not remeasured for equity-classified awards.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 718-10-35-2により、株式決済のRSUは持分分類のアワードである。報酬費用総額 = 5,000 × $20 = $100,000を3年の確定期間にわたり認識。年間費用 = $100,000 / 3 = $33,333。公正価値は付与日に測定し、持分分類のアワードでは再測定しない。

questions.stem: Entity D offers an ESPP that allows employees to purchase shares at a 10% discount from market price. The plan meets the criteria for a noncompensatory plan under ASC 718. Which of the following is a requirement for noncompensatory classification?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The discount must not exceed 5% of the market price

choices.label: B
choices.text: The discount must not exceed 15% of the market price

choices.label: C
choices.text: The plan must be available only to senior executives

choices.label: D
choices.text: The discount must be at least 20% of the market price

questions.explanation: Under ASC 718-10-25-16, an ESPP is noncompensatory if, among other criteria, the discount from market price does not exceed the per-share cost savings from issuing directly to employees (typically 5%). A 10% discount would not meet this criterion and would be compensatory, requiring expense recognition. The question tests knowledge of the 5% safe harbor.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 718-10-25-16により、ESPPが非報酬型であるためには、市場価格からの割引が従業員への直接発行による1株当たりコスト削減額（通常5%）を超えないこと等の基準を満たす必要がある。10%割引はこの基準を満たさず報酬型となり、費用認識が必要となる。

questions.stem: Entity E issues 10,000 stock warrants to existing shareholders as a stock dividend. Each warrant entitles the holder to purchase one share at $25 (current market price is $30). The fair value of each warrant is $8. How should Entity E record the issuance?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Debit Retained Earnings $80,000; Credit Warrants Outstanding $80,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: No entry because warrants issued as dividends have no cost

choices.label: C
choices.text: Debit Retained Earnings $80,000; Credit APIC $80,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Debit Expense $80,000; Credit Warrants Outstanding $80,000

questions.explanation: When stock warrants are distributed as a dividend to existing shareholders, retained earnings is debited for the fair value of the warrants distributed (10,000 × $8 = $80,000), and a separate equity account (Warrants Outstanding) is credited. This is similar to a property dividend measured at fair value.
questions.explanationJa: 株式ワラントを既存株主への配当として分配する場合、分配したワラントの公正価値（10,000 × $8 = $80,000）で利益剰余金を借方に計上し、別個の資本勘定（ワラント未行使残高）を貸方に計上する。公正価値で測定する財産配当と同様である。

questions.stem: Entity F acquires 2,000 shares of its own stock at $40 per share. The shares were originally issued at $15 par value with $10 APIC per share. Entity F uses the constructive retirement method. What is the journal entry?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Debit Treasury Stock $80,000; Credit Cash $80,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Debit Common Stock $30,000; Debit APIC $20,000; Debit Retained Earnings $30,000; Credit Cash $80,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Debit Retained Earnings $80,000; Credit Cash $80,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Debit Common Stock $30,000; Debit APIC $50,000; Credit Cash $80,000

questions.explanation: Under the constructive retirement method (par value method), the treasury stock transaction is treated as if the shares are retired. Common Stock = 2,000 × $15 = $30,000. APIC = 2,000 × $10 = $20,000. Excess of cost over original issuance = $80,000 − $30,000 − $20,000 = $30,000, debited to Retained Earnings.
questions.explanationJa: 推定消却法（額面法）では、自己株式取引を株式が消却されたかのように処理する。普通株式 = 2,000 × $15 = $30,000。APIC = 2,000 × $10 = $20,000。取得原価と当初発行価額の超過額 = $80,000 − $30,000 − $20,000 = $30,000を利益剰余金の借方に計上。

questions.stem: Entity G has 10,000 shares of 6%, $100 par cumulative preferred stock outstanding. No dividends were declared in Year 1 or Year 2. In Year 3, Entity G declares a total dividend of $250,000. How much of the dividend goes to preferred shareholders?
questions.explanation: Annual preferred dividend = 10,000 × $100 × 6% = $60,000. Cumulative dividends in arrears: Year 1 ($60,000) + Year 2 ($60,000) = $120,000. Year 3 current year = $60,000. Total preferred claim = $120,000 + $60,000 = $180,000. Since $250,000 > $180,000, preferred receives $180,000 and common receives $70,000.

questions.stem: Entity H modifies stock options held by employees by reducing the exercise price from $50 to $35. The original fair value of the options was $12 per option, and the fair value immediately after modification is $18 per option. There are 1,000 options with 2 years of remaining vesting. What incremental compensation cost should be recognized?

choices.label: A
choices.text: $6,000 over the remaining 2 years

choices.label: B
choices.text: $18,000 over the remaining 2 years

choices.label: C
choices.text: $6,000 immediately

choices.label: D
choices.text: $12,000 over the remaining 2 years

questions.explanation: Under ASC 718-20-35-3, when a modification increases fair value, the incremental compensation cost equals the excess of the fair value of the modified award over the fair value of the original award immediately before modification. Incremental cost = ($18 − $12) × 1,000 = $6,000, recognized over the remaining 2-year vesting period.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 718-20-35-3により、公正価値を増加させる修正では、増分報酬費用は修正直前の原アワードの公正価値に対する修正後アワードの公正価値の超過額となる。増分費用 = ($18 − $12) × 1,000 = $6,000を残りの2年の確定期間にわたり認識する。

questions.stem: Entity J has 100,000 common shares and 20,000 participating preferred shares. Net income is $600,000, and the preferred stock participates in dividends with common shares on a 1:1 basis after receiving a $2 per share base dividend. Total dividends declared are $200,000. What is the EPS using the two-class method?
questions.explanation: Preferred base dividend = 20,000 × $2 = $40,000. Remaining dividends = $200,000 − $40,000 = $160,000, shared 100,000:20,000 = 5:1. Common share of remaining dividends = $160,000 × 5/6 = $133,333. Undistributed earnings = $600,000 − $200,000 = $400,000, shared 5:1. Common share = $400,000 × 5/6 = $333,333. Common EPS = ($133,333 + $333,333) / 100,000 = $4.67.

questions.stem: Entity K issues preferred shares that must be redeemed by the issuer at a fixed price of $100 per share on a specified date. Under ASC 480, how should these shares be classified?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As equity because they are shares of stock

choices.label: B
choices.text: As a liability because they embody an unconditional obligation to redeem

choices.label: C
choices.text: As mezzanine (temporary) equity

choices.label: D
choices.text: As equity with a disclosure of the redemption feature

questions.explanation: Under ASC 480-10-25-4, mandatorily redeemable financial instruments that embody an unconditional obligation requiring the issuer to redeem the instrument at a specified date are classified as liabilities. The fixed redemption price creates a liability, and changes in fair value or accretion are recognized in income.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 480-10-25-4により、発行者が特定の日に金融商品を償還する無条件の義務を有する強制償還金融商品は負債に分類する。固定の償還価格が負債を創出し、公正価値変動または増加額は損益で認識する。

questions.stem: Entity L has 5,000 shares of $100 par convertible preferred stock. Each preferred share converts to 4 common shares ($10 par). The preferred stock was originally issued at $110 per share. All 5,000 preferred shares are converted. What is the journal entry?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Debit Preferred Stock $500,000; Debit APIC-Preferred $50,000; Credit Common Stock $200,000; Credit APIC-Common $350,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Debit Preferred Stock $500,000; Credit Common Stock $200,000; Credit Gain on Conversion $300,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Debit Preferred Stock $550,000; Credit Common Stock $200,000; Credit APIC-Common $350,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Debit Preferred Stock $500,000; Debit APIC-Preferred $50,000; Credit Common Stock $550,000

questions.explanation: Upon conversion, the book value method is used. Remove preferred stock at carrying amount: Preferred Stock ($500,000) + APIC-Preferred ($50,000) = $550,000. Issue common shares: 5,000 × 4 = 20,000 shares × $10 par = $200,000 to Common Stock. Remainder = $550,000 − $200,000 = $350,000 to APIC-Common. No gain/loss on conversion.
questions.explanationJa: 転換時は帳簿価額法を使用。優先株式の帳簿価額を除去：優先株式($500,000) + APIC-優先($50,000) = $550,000。普通株式を発行：5,000 × 4 = 20,000株 × $10額面 = $200,000を普通株式に。残額 = $550,000 − $200,000 = $350,000をAPIC-普通に。転換による損益は認識しない。

questions.stem: Entity M grants 10,000 stock options with a performance condition: options vest only if revenue exceeds $50 million in Year 3. The grant-date fair value is $5 per option. At the end of Year 1, management believes it is probable (70% likely) that the target will be met. What compensation expense should be recognized in Year 1?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 718-10-30-27, performance conditions are not reflected in the grant-date fair value but affect the quantity of awards expected to vest. If probable of achievement, total cost = 10,000 × $5 = $50,000 over 3 years. Year 1 expense = $50,000 × 1/3 = $16,667. If the condition is not probable, no expense is recognized.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 718-10-30-27により、業績条件は付与日公正価値には反映されないが、確定見込みのアワード数に影響する。達成が見込まれる場合、総費用 = 10,000 × $5 = $50,000を3年にわたり認識。Year 1の費用 = $50,000 × 1/3 = $16,667。条件が見込まれない場合、費用は認識しない。

questions.stem: Entity N grants stock options that vest only if the stock price exceeds $50 within 3 years. The grant-date fair value (which incorporates the market condition) is $3 per option for 20,000 options. After Year 2, the stock price has not exceeded $50 and is unlikely to do so. Should Entity N reverse previously recognized compensation expense?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Yes, reverse all expense because vesting is unlikely

choices.label: B
choices.text: No, compensation expense is recognized regardless of whether the market condition is met

choices.label: C
choices.text: Yes, but only the Year 2 expense

choices.label: D
choices.text: No expense should have been recognized from the start

questions.explanation: Under ASC 718-10-30-19, market conditions are incorporated into the grant-date fair value measurement. Unlike performance conditions, the probability of achieving a market condition does not affect expense recognition. Compensation expense is recognized over the requisite service period regardless of whether the market condition is ultimately satisfied.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 718-10-30-19により、市場条件は付与日公正価値の測定に織り込まれる。業績条件とは異なり、市場条件の達成確率は費用認識に影響しない。報酬費用は市場条件が最終的に満たされるかどうかにかかわらず、必要なサービス期間にわたり認識する。

questions.stem: Entity P has accumulated other comprehensive income (AOCI) with the following components: unrealized gain on AFS securities $30,000, foreign currency translation adjustment ($15,000), and pension prior service cost ($20,000). Entity P sells all AFS securities. What reclassification adjustment is required?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Reclassify $30,000 from AOCI to net income

choices.label: B
choices.text: Reclassify all $5,000 net AOCI to net income

choices.label: C
choices.text: No reclassification is needed

choices.label: D
choices.text: Reclassify $30,000 from AOCI to retained earnings

questions.explanation: Under ASC 220-10-45-15, when AFS securities are sold, the unrealized gain previously reported in AOCI ($30,000) is reclassified to net income (realized gain). The foreign currency translation adjustment and pension prior service cost remain in AOCI as they have not been realized or otherwise triggered for reclassification.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 220-10-45-15により、AFS証券が売却された場合、AOCIに報告されていた未実現利益($30,000)は純利益に再分類される（実現利益）。外貨換算調整と年金過去勤務費用は実現またはその他の再分類トリガーがないため、AOCIに残る。

questions.subtopic: Par Value vs No-Par Stock Issuance
questions.stem: Entity Q issues 10,000 shares of no-par common stock with a stated value of $5 per share for $30 per share. What is the journal entry?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Debit Cash $300,000; Credit Common Stock $300,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Debit Cash $300,000; Credit Common Stock $50,000; Credit APIC $250,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Debit Cash $300,000; Credit Common Stock $100,000; Credit APIC $200,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Debit Cash $300,000; Credit Revenue $300,000

questions.explanation: For no-par stock with a stated value, the stated value ($5 per share) is credited to Common Stock: 10,000 × $5 = $50,000. The excess over stated value is credited to APIC: ($30 − $5) × 10,000 = $250,000. If there were no stated value, the entire $300,000 would be credited to Common Stock.
questions.explanationJa: 額面のない株式で設定額がある場合、設定額($5/株)を普通株式に貸方計上：10,000 × $5 = $50,000。設定額を超える部分をAPICに貸方計上：($30 − $5) × 10,000 = $250,000。設定額がなければ$300,000全額を普通株式に計上する。

questions.stem: Entity R receives subscriptions for 5,000 shares of $10 par common stock at $25 per share. Subscribers pay 40% down payment. How should Entity R record the subscription?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Debit Cash $50,000; Debit Subscription Receivable $75,000; Credit Common Stock Subscribed $50,000; Credit APIC $75,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Debit Cash $50,000; Credit Common Stock $50,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Debit Cash $125,000; Credit Common Stock $50,000; Credit APIC $75,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Debit Cash $50,000; Debit Subscription Receivable $75,000; Credit Common Stock $125,000

questions.explanation: Total subscription = 5,000 × $25 = $125,000. Down payment = 40% × $125,000 = $50,000. Subscription Receivable = $75,000. Common Stock Subscribed (at par) = 5,000 × $10 = $50,000. APIC = ($25 − $10) × 5,000 = $75,000. Shares are issued when fully paid; until then, Common Stock Subscribed is reported in equity.

questions.stem: Entity S, a mining company, declares a dividend of $5 per share. Entity S informs shareholders that $3 represents a return of capital (liquidating dividend) and $2 is from earnings. Entity S has 50,000 shares outstanding. What are the journal entries?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Debit Retained Earnings $100,000; Debit APIC $150,000; Credit Dividends Payable $250,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Debit Retained Earnings $250,000; Credit Dividends Payable $250,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Debit APIC $250,000; Credit Dividends Payable $250,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Debit Retained Earnings $150,000; Debit APIC $100,000; Credit Dividends Payable $250,000

questions.explanation: Earnings portion: $2 × 50,000 = $100,000 debited to Retained Earnings. Return of capital (liquidating dividend): $3 × 50,000 = $150,000 debited to APIC (or paid-in capital). Total Dividends Payable = $250,000. Shareholders must be informed of the portion that represents a return of capital.
questions.explanationJa: 利益部分：$2 × 50,000 = $100,000を利益剰余金の借方に計上。資本の返還（清算配当）：$3 × 50,000 = $150,000をAPICの借方に計上。配当金支払義務合計 = $250,000。株主には資本の返還部分を通知しなければならない。

questions.stem: Entity T grants 1,000 stock appreciation rights (SARs) to be settled in cash. The SARs vest over 2 years. At the end of Year 1, the fair value of each SAR is $15. At the end of Year 2, the fair value is $20. What is the cumulative compensation expense at the end of Year 2?
questions.explanation: Cash-settled SARs are liability-classified and remeasured at fair value each period. Year 1 expense = 1,000 × $15 × 1/2 = $7,500. Year 2 cumulative = 1,000 × $20 × 2/2 = $20,000. Year 2 expense = $20,000 − $7,500 = $12,500. Total cumulative expense at end of Year 2 = $20,000.
questions.explanationJa: 現金決済のSARは負債分類で各期に公正価値で再測定する。Year 1費用 = 1,000 × $15 × 1/2 = $7,500。Year 2累計 = 1,000 × $20 × 2/2 = $20,000。Year 2費用 = $20,000 − $7,500 = $12,500。Year 2末の累積費用合計 = $20,000。

questions.stem: Parent Company U distributes the shares of its wholly owned Subsidiary V to its shareholders as a spin-off. The carrying amount of Subsidiary V's net assets is $5,000,000. Under ASC 505-60, at what amount should Parent U record the distribution?

choices.label: A
choices.text: At the fair value of Subsidiary V

choices.label: B
choices.text: At the carrying amount of Subsidiary V's net assets ($5,000,000)

choices.label: C
choices.text: At the par value of Subsidiary V's stock

choices.label: D
choices.text: At zero since no consideration is received

questions.explanation: Under ASC 505-60-25-2, a spin-off distribution to shareholders of a controlled entity is generally recorded at the carrying amount (book value) of the subsidiary's net assets. The distribution reduces retained earnings (or APIC) by the carrying amount. No gain or loss is recognized by the parent on the distribution.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 505-60-25-2により、支配する企業の株主へのスピンオフ分配は、一般に子会社の純資産の帳簿価額で計上する。分配は帳簿価額分だけ利益剰余金（またはAPIC）を減少させる。親会社は分配による損益を認識しない。

questions.stem: Entity W has 200,000 common shares outstanding and net income of $500,000. Entity W will issue 10,000 additional shares if revenue exceeds $10 million in Year 2. At the end of Year 1, current revenue is $8 million. Should the contingently issuable shares be included in diluted EPS for Year 1?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Yes, always include contingently issuable shares in diluted EPS

choices.label: B
choices.text: No, because the revenue condition has not been met as of the reporting date

choices.label: C
choices.text: Yes, if the condition would be met assuming the end of the reporting period is the end of the contingency period

choices.label: D
choices.text: No, contingently issuable shares are never included in diluted EPS

questions.explanation: Under ASC 260-10-45-48, contingently issuable shares are included in diluted EPS if the conditions would have been satisfied assuming the end of the reporting period were the end of the contingency period. Since revenue of $8 million at year-end does not meet the $10 million target, the shares would NOT be included in Year 1 diluted EPS under this test.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 260-10-45-48により、報告期間の末日が条件付期間の末日であると仮定した場合に条件が満たされるならば、条件付発行可能株式を希薄化後EPSに含める。年末の売上$800万は目標$1,000万を満たさないため、このテストではYear 1の希薄化後EPSに含めない。
